2604.25423.title: Do LLMs Capture Embodied Cognition and Cultural Variation? Cross-Linguistic Evidence from Demonstratives
2604.25423.paper_summary: Do large language models (LLMs) truly acquire embodied cognition and cultural conventions from text? We introduce demonstratives, fundamental spatial expressions like "this/that" in English and "zhè/nà" in Chinese, as a novel probe for grounded knowledge. Using 6,400 responses from 320 native speakers, we establish a human baseline: English speakers reliably distinguish proximal-distal referents but struggle with perspective-taking, while Chinese speakers switch perspectives fluently but tolerate distal ambiguity. In contrast, five state-of-the-art LLMs fail to inherently understand the proximal-distal contrast and show no cultural differences, defaulting to English-centric reasoning. Our study contributes (i) a new task, based on demonstratives, as a new lens for evaluating embodied cognition and cultural conventions; (ii) empirical evidence of cross-cultural asymmetries in human interpretation; (iii) a new perspective on the egocentric-sociocentric debate, showing both orientations coexist but vary across languages; and (iv) a call to address individual variation in future model design.
2604.25423.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）是否真的從文本中獲得了具身認知和文化慣例？我們引入指示詞，像英語中的 "this/that" 和中文中的 "zhè/nà" 這樣的基本空間表達，作為檢驗具體知識的新工具。通過分析320名母語者的6,400個回應，我們建立了人類基準：英語使用者可靠地區分近遠指稱，但在視角轉換上存在困難，而中文使用者則能流利地切換視角，但對遠指稱的模糊性則能容忍。相比之下，五個最先進的LLMs無法內在理解近遠對比，且未顯示出文化差異，默認使用以英語為中心的推理。我們的研究貢獻了（i）一項基於指示詞的新任務，作為評估具身認知和文化慣例的新視角；（ii）人類解釋中的跨文化不對稱的實證證據；（iii）對自我中心與社會中心辯論的新看法，顯示這兩種取向共存但在不同語言中有所變化；以及（iv）呼籲在未來的模型設計中考慮個體差異。
2604.25423.author: Yu Wang et.al.
2604.25423.authors: Yu Wang, Emmanuele Chersoni, Chu-Ren Huang
